acceptanceSignals: TOTP QR verification is single-use and replay-protected.
Invalid or expired code scans are rejected with auditable events.
Peak verification load tests meet locked SLO targets before release.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Pickup TOTP Check-in in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The current Pickup TOTP Check-in workspace satisfies the active HTTP contract policy: externally visible pickup verification behavior changes are reflected in `contract/openapi` (JSON+YAML), synchronized generated TypeScript client artifacts, and contract guardrails (route-to-spec coverage plus error-code/schema assertions). Contract drift check passes in this workspace.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The current workspace satisfies the Pickup TOTP Check-in policy: pickup verification is TOTP-QR-only, replay is blocked via single-use fulfillment state transitions, invalid/expired scans emit auditable rejection events, Asia/Taipei fixed-time semantics are enforced for token steps, and release is gated by blocking hard-SLO load checks (including peak pickup verification) wired into CI. No active legacy/fallback pickup route/DTO/handler path was found.

reinforcementResults.name: Peak Load SLO Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The feature satisfies the active policy: pickup verification is implemented as TOTP QR-only with replay protection via single-use fulfillment state transitions, invalid/expired scans are rejected and emitted as audit events, and hard pre-launch SLO gating is enforced with committed peak order+pickup load evidence and Grafana/alert artifacts.

excludedApproaches: Fallback production flow using manual PIN/name check as primary path.
Operating without hard performance acceptance thresholds.
executionConstraints: Use TOTP QR as the sole production pickup verification mechanism.
Apply fixed Asia/Taipei time semantics for token validity boundaries.
Block release until hard throughput/latency/error-rate SLO checks pass.
name: Pickup TOTP Check-in
rationale: Pickup speed and correctness are critical frontline operational risks.
summary: Provide secure high-throughput on-site verification and meal claim updates.
